content.problemStatement:
You are given an array of integers. Your task is to write a C program that uses a pointer and pointer arithmetic to traverse the array and print each element.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer `n`, representing the size of the array.
The second line contains `n` space-separated integers, representing the elements of the array.


Output Format

Print each element of the array on a new line using pointer arithmetic.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer n (1 ≤ n ≤ 100), the size of the array; the second line contains n space‑separated integers arr[i] (0 ≤ arr[i] ≤ 1000).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the n elements of the array in the order they appear, each on its own line. Outputs of consecutive test cases follow one another without extra blank lines.

sampleTestCases.explanation: One test case with 5 elements; each element is printed on its own line in the given order.
Single test case with three elements; they are output sequentially.

solution.approach: We simply read the input using fast I/O, then for each test case we traverse the array using a pointer (or its equivalent in Java) and output each element. Since we only need to print the elements in the given order, no extra processing is required. The algorithm runs in linear time with respect to the total number of elements and uses O(1) additional memory besides the input storage.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the size of the array; O(total N) for the whole input.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space (apart from the input array which is required to read the numbers).

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());while(T-- > 0){int n=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());for(int i=0;i<n;i++){sb.append(st.nextToken()).append('\n');}}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
